proxy: D1: EBS school-age teachers per 1,000 students
note: Predetermined birth-aimag school supply during ages 6-17; uses obs with >=3 EBS years.

proxy: D2: EBS school-age schools per 1,000 students
note: Predetermined school access during ages 6-17; uses obs with >=3 EBS years.
Predetermined class-size/school-quality proxy during ages 6-17; uses obs with >=3 EBS years.

proxy: D4: EBS age-12 teachers per 1,000 students
note: Exact birth-aimag supply at age 12; smaller young-cohort sample.

proxy: D5: EBS age-12 schools per 1,000 students
note: Exact birth-aimag school access at age 12; smaller young-cohort sample.
Exact birth-aimag class-size/school-quality proxy at age 12; smaller young-cohort sample.
Predetermined regional supply in 2000; full sample but not older cohorts' own school-age exposure.
Predetermined regional school access in 2000; full sample but looser cohort interpretation.
Predetermined regional class-size/school-quality proxy in 2000; full sample but looser cohort interpretation.
Fully predetermined; tests cohort heterogeneity rather than education-access threshold.

proxy: C3: 2008 reform cohort dummy (effectively binary; born >= 1991)
note: Cohort exposure to 2008 12-year system. The grid trim leaves only one cut, so this is reported as a cohort-difference test, not a continuous CH threshold.
Alternative cohort-state version of reform exposure.
Predetermined relative to person but constructed from HSES schooling outcomes.
Outcome-based peer/regional education proxy; not cleanly exogenous.
Predetermined geography but may directly proxy labor-market access.
Same variable is endogenous regressor and threshold; violates CH exogenous-threshold assumption.
